Empires & Puzzles Revna Review: The Carnelian Hunter's Guide

Revna might not have the raw muscle of the 2026 god-tier heroes, but her ability to reflect Wither and safely cleanse makes her a tactical nuke against specific teams. We analyze if she still has a spot on your roster.
Revna: The Carnelian Hunter Who Reflects Your Bad Day
Revna, The Carnelian Hunter: Reflecting Wither and Woe

Revna arrived in October 2024 as part of the Plains Hunter (Monster Hunter) family, bringing a very specific set of tools to the Fire roster. She's a Fast mana Cleric who doesn't just clean up messes—she mails them back to the sender. While she doesn't have a costume yet, her base form offers a unique "safe cleanse" and reflection mechanic that keeps her relevant even as her stats start to show their age compared to the behemoths of 2026. Think of her as the red Hathor with a bit more attitude and a lot more feathers.
Revna sports a "Carnelian Hunter" aesthetic that sits somewhere between

. She's decked out in a cloak of red and teal feathers that matches her "Cloak of Feathers" skill perfectly. The art is dynamic, with a great color contrast between the earthy reds and the bright teal accents. It’s a solid, grounded design that fits the Monster Hunter theme without looking like she just raided a Spirit Halloween store.

At 1107 base power (before Limit Breaks), Revna is officially living in the "Vintage" section of your hero roster. Compared to modern 2026 heroes who are pushing 1260+ Power, she is significantly behind the curve. Her 2115 Health and ~1090 defenses were tank-worthy in late 2024, but today she is fragile if left untouched. To make her viable in top-tier play, she absolutely requires Limit Breaking (both LB1 and LB2) and full emblems to survive the initial volley of modern fast snipers.
This skill is the reason you still use Revna.

A flat percentage heal is decent, but at 35%, it's merely a "top-up" in today's meta of overhealers and 50%+ restorers. It keeps you alive, but it won't bring you back from the brink of death like a slow healer would.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Plains Hunter family is a bit stingy, requiring 2+ heroes for the bonus, but the passives are where Revna shines individually.
The Attack bonus (5%/10%/15%/20%) is nice but largely irrelevant for a support healer. You aren't bringing Revna for her tile damage; you're bringing her to keep your team purple-free.
A 10% chance to drop received special damage to 1. It's a low probability (RNG) save, but when it happens, it feels like winning the lottery. It adds a layer of "annoyance" to her defense.
Adds a small DoT (150 dmg) to a random enemy. It's flavor text at this point; 150 damage in 2026 is negligible, but it can occasionally finish off a hero hanging on by 1 HP.
is the perfect Aether Power for her. Since her main weakness is her aging stat line, a flat defense boost at the start of battle helps ensure she lives long enough to cast her reflection.
Upon release, Revna was hailed as a "Fast Hathor" or an "Anti-Goblin" specialist because of her Wither reflection. She was a premium counter to the Goblin meta that dominated late 2023/2024.
Her stats have fallen off. A 1107 Power hero is now food for the 1260+ monsters. However, utility often outlives stats. Just like Rigard is still used years later, Revna's unique

. See a Goblin team? Bring Revna. See a heavy ailment stacker? Bring Revna. She has moved from a main tank/flank role to an offensive specialist role.

Revna is best used as a proactive counter. Don't just bring her for healing; bring her because you want the enemy to hurt themselves.
Revna + Azurite + Other Reflectors. Stack elemental reflects and status reflects to make the enemy afraid to press any buttons.
Revna + heavy hitters. Use Revna to bounce back the inevitable Wither from Acidfire/Darkfeather, effectively using their own strength to weaken them.
Her reflect is a buff. Anyone who dispels first (e.g., Hippo, many Ninjas) removes her protection immediately.
If she is blocked, she can't cast her reflect or heal.
Heroes who dump 3-4 ailments at once (like some modern Cubes) will laugh at her "Cleanse 1" ability.
If you pulled her, congrats! She is an amazing healer for your stage of the game. Prioritize her. The reflect mechanic will help you cheese through hard map stages where bosses spam status effects.

She is a tool for your toolbox, not a daily driver. Do not put her on defense unless it's a very specific Rush setup; she's too easy to play around or snipe out. Use her on offense to counter Wither/Stack teams. If you have newer, beefier healers (like 2026 meta healers), she might stay on the bench until a Goblin war pops up.
Many players immediately compared her to Hathor. While Hathor
. The community generally agrees that Reflect is more fun/punishing, but Prevention is safer.
There was confusion about what "Safely Cleanses" meant. It was clarified that she doesn't take damage from "dispelling" effects, which is a subtle but important interaction.
One YouTuber was shocked to find she reflected the "Dance" ailment from Owls. It turns out, if it's a status ailment, Revna sends it back!
Revna is the kind of hero that ages gracefully because her skill is unique. Stats rot, damage numbers get crept, but

is a mechanic that will likely remain useful for years. She isn't the shiny new toy anymore, but when you're staring down a team of Goblins trying to Wither you into dust, Revna is the one you want in your corner.
hero in 2026. Keep her, level her, limit break her if you hate Goblins.
